functional_requirements.title: Add Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can add an expense record with amount, category, description, and date. Each record gets a unique ID. Amount must be positive. Date defaults to today. Categories: food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other.

functional_requirements.title: List Expenses
functional_requirements.description: User can list all expense records with optional filtering by category and date range (start_date, end_date). Output shows ID, amount, category, description, date.

functional_requirements.title: Update Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can modify an expense record by ID. Fields: amount, category, description. Check if record exists.

functional_requirements.title: Delete Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can delete an expense record by ID. Check if record exists.

functional_requirements.title: Get Summary
functional_requirements.description: User can get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range. Returns total amount and amounts per category.

non_functional_requirements.description: All operations must be accessible via a single function call track_expense(command, expense_data).
Error handling for invalid ID, negative amount, invalid category, and invalid date range.

acceptance_criteria.description: Add expense with valid data returns success and unique ID.
Add expense with negative amount returns error.
List expenses without filters returns all records.
List expenses with category filter returns only matching records.
List expenses with date range filter returns records within range.
Update expense with valid ID updates fields and returns success.
Update expense with non-existent ID returns error.
Delete expense with valid ID removes record and returns success.
Delete expense with non-existent ID returns error.
Summary grouped by category returns correct totals.
Summary with date range returns correct totals within range.

storage.details: Data stored in memory during session, no persistence.

glossary.expense_id: Unique identifier for each expense record.
glossary.category: Expense category: food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other.
glossary.amount: Expense amount as float with two decimal places.

global_state_sources.description: Counter for generating unique expense IDs.

input_spec.description: Input is passed as arguments to the track_expense function: command (string) and expense_data (dict).

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object returned by track_expense.
